title: swtpm env-var init is a process-global footgun with inadequate documentation
description: Both device.rs and connection.rs temporarily set SWTPM_SERVER_NAME/PORT in the process environment to pass the swtpm host/port to wolfTPM2_Init, which reads them internally. The mutex only serializes callers through the Rust wrapper. A user who sets SWTPM_SERVER_NAME externally before running (e.g. in a test harness) will have it clobbered silently. A user who reads the env var from another thread not holding the lock will see a transient value. The doc comment warns 'does not protect against unrelated threads', but does not tell users what they should do instead (e.g. do not rely on SWTPM_SERVER_NAME from the env; set it via this API only). This will generate a steady stream of hard-to-reproduce bug reports from users running parallel test suites.
